component.name: SynchroSonic
component.summary: Linux-first LAN audio casting and receiver control

description.p: Automated Linux packaging release for the Rust/GTK SynchroSonic desktop app on Linux.

This release adds AppImage, Debian package, Flatpak bundle, and portable tarball distribution formats with automated CI builds. Includes LAN discovery, system-audio capture, receiver mode, synchronized multi-target casting, diagnostics, and complete Linux packaging automation.

SynchroSonic is a Linux-first GTK4/libadwaita desktop application for capturing system audio, discovering receivers, casting over the local network, running a receiver mode, and inspecting stream, sync, and buffer health.

The current release track focuses on Linux PipeWire tooling, LAN transport, and explicit diagnostics. Bluetooth support is intentionally limited to selecting local Linux playback outputs exposed by PipeWire.
The current public tag target is a release candidate rather than a stable release because final signed packaging and a published private security reporting path are not in place yet.
